summary: User Lookup | Support Human Readable Dates in User Lookup
description:
As a User-Lookup tool user
I want to see session and playback session dates in a human-readable format
So that I can quickly understand when events occurred without having to manually parse UTC time strings

[https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/OPTOOLS-1214|https://baseball.atlassian.net/browse/OPTOOLS-1214|smart-link]
gherkin:
When a user is logged into the "Streaming Operator Platform" and navigates to the "User Lookup" page
And the user enters a valid Okta ID or Email, such as "dm5tv2@gmail.com" in the Enter User ID or Email box
And the user clicks on the "Search" button
Then the user should see each session's "Init Time" and "Expired Date" in a human-readable format:
Format Example:
Monday April 2nd, 2025 1:50 PM (UTC)
And the displayed format should include:
Day of the week | Month | Day | Year | Time | Time Zone
And all displayed times should be in UTC by default

steps.action:
*When* a user is logged into the "Streaming Operator Platform" and navigates to the "User Lookup" page

*And* the user enters a valid Okta ID or Email, such as "[dm5tv2@gmail.com|mailto:dMdv2@gmail.com]" in the Enter User ID or Email box 

*And* the user clicks on the "Search" button
steps.result:
*Then* the user should see each session's "Init Time" and "Expired Date" in a human-readable format:

Format Example:

Monday April 2nd, 2025 1:50 PM (UTC) 

*And* the displayed format should include:

Day of the week | Month | Day | Year | Time | Time Zone

*And* all displayed times should be in UTC by default
